techcrunch.title: Ahead of its IPO, Anthropic’s Daniela Amodei shrugs off doubts about AI’s returns
techcrunch.description: The AI giant's co-founder explained why the company may tap the public market for capital and why tokenmaxxing pushback isn't a concern.

techcrunch.title: Airbnb’s Brian Chesky plans to launch a new AI lab
techcrunch.description: The Airbnb CEO said last year it hasn't struck an LLM partnership because existing products weren't quite ready.

techcrunch.title: Filtr is a new privacy tool that blocks ads in almost every iPhone and Mac app
techcrunch.description: This popular ad blocker app for iPhones, iPads, and Macs can now block ads from loading inside apps, including web browsers, thanks to a new feature in the latest Apple software.

techcrunch.title: Defense tech, AI, and fundraising take center stage at StrictlyVC Los Angeles on June 18
techcrunch.description: On Thursday, June 18, at The Aerospace Corporation Campus, investors, founders, and tech leaders will gather for an evening of conversation exploring some of the most consequential shifts taking place across venture capital, defense technology, artificial intelligence, and advanced industry. Secure your spot today.

techcrunch.title: Startup Battlefield is returning to Australia — here’s what happened the last time we came to Sydney
techcrunch.description: On August 19, Startup Battlefield is returning to Sydney in partnership with Stripe, one of the world's most iconic technology companies. We're taking over Stripe Tour Sydney for a night that the Australian startup ecosystem won't forget.

techcrunch.title: Meta steals a tactic from Tesla and builds data centers in tents
techcrunch.description: Meta may have found one way to slash its massive data center bill: tents.

techcrunch.title: Apple approves Poke as the first AI agent on its Messages for Business platform
techcrunch.description: Poke, the startup that lets people use AI agents through simple text messages, has become the first AI agent approved for Apple’s Messages for Business platform.

techcrunch.title: Helion, the Sam Altman-backed fusion startup, raises $465M to build a power plant for Microsoft
techcrunch.description: Fusion startup Helion is racing to complete a power plant for Microsoft by 2028. A fresh infusion of cash should help with that.

techcrunch.title: Meta’s Oversight Board says account bans lack due process, transparency
techcrunch.description: Meta's board cites "due process" concerns over account bans. It's also pushing Meta to offer clear information about violations and its use in AI in making its determinations.

techcrunch.title: Meta rolls out a new AI creator assistant on Facebook
techcrunch.description: Creators often have to parse through charts and dashboards to understand their performance, but with the new AI assistant, they can get quick answers to questions like "When should I post?" and "What are people saying in my comments?"

techcrunch.title: What to expect from WWDC 2026: Siri’s highly anticipated revamp and Apple Intelligence updates
techcrunch.description: Apple's WWDC nears: Here's what you can look forward to.

techcrunch.title: A burglar used a Waymo to steal yoga clothes in San Francisco — and got away with it
techcrunch.description: The incident helps shed some new light on how Waymo treats and stores the footage captured by its robotaxis.

techcrunch.title: Cash App launches a wand for tap-and-pay
techcrunch.description: Digital wallet app Cash App is launching a new gadget on Thursday, seemingly inspired by the social media trend that involves paying for items in the real world with a tap of a homemade magic wand, which hides a tap-and-pay credit card.

techcrunch.title: Waymo’s spent robotaxi batteries will be used as grid storage
techcrunch.description: The company announced a deal with B2U Storage Solutions to repurpose the battery packs as Waymo pulls them off the road.

techcrunch.title: Ramp raises $750M at $44B valuation as investors hunger for fintechs with an AI story
techcrunch.description: Ramp has nearly tripled its valuation over the past year as investors scramble to grab a part of the fast-growing startup.

techcrunch.title: Is Silicon Valley ready to put robots in people’s homes? Hello Robot is.
techcrunch.description: The California startup released the fourth-generation of its home assistance robot, Stretch.

techcrunch.title: Chinese spies are using LinkedIn to lure Westerners into sharing sensitive information
techcrunch.description: The advisory warns that Chinese spies are using public job search platforms to recruit people with access to non-public information.

techcrunch.title: Apple touts $1.4 trillion in App Store billings and sales, 90% without a commission
techcrunch.description: Apple's App Store generated $1.4 trillion in sales, up from $1.3 trillion last year, with $149 billion in sales for digital goods.

techcrunch.title: Oura Ring 5 review: Thinner, lighter, better
techcrunch.description: The Ring 5, which Oura describes as the world’s smallest smart ring, is 40% smaller than its predecessor and starts at $399.

techcrunch.title: Benchmark raises its first-ever growth fund as part of $2B capital haul
techcrunch.description: The legendary Silicon Valley VC firm abandons its more than 20-year tradition of keeping its funds to about $425 million.

mit_review.description: The global health care sector is under increasing strain.  Decades of chronic underinvestment and constraints in recruitment have coincided with a surge in demand for services for aging populations. Gaps in provision are already taking a toll, with fragmented access to care and high rates of stress and burnout among staff. And it’s getting worse.…
